questions.question: What is the formula for sodium chloride?
questions.explanation: Sodium chloride, also known as table salt, has a chemical formula of NaCl.

questions.question: What is the charge on an aluminum ion?
What is the chemical formula for calcium oxide?
questions.explanation: Calcium oxide has a chemical formula of CaO.

questions.question: What is the charge on a magnesium ion?
What is the formula for magnesium chloride?
questions.explanation: Magnesium chloride has a chemical formula of MgCl2.

questions.question: What is the atomic number of chlorine?
questions.explanation: Chlorine has an atomic number of 17.

questions.question: What is the charge on a sodium ion?
What is the formula for calcium carbonate?
questions.explanation: Calcium carbonate has a chemical formula of CaCO3.

questions.question: What is the charge on an iron ion?
questions.explanation: Iron ions can have either a +2 or +3 charge.

questions.question: What is the chemical formula for sodium sulfide?
questions.explanation: Sodium sulfide has a chemical formula of Na2S.

notes.title: Atomic Structure
notes.content: Atoms are the building blocks of matter and consist of protons, neutrons, and electrons. Protons have a positive charge, neutrons have no charge, and electrons have a negative charge.

notes.title: Chemical Bonding
notes.content: Chemical bonding occurs when atoms share or exchange electrons to form molecules. There are three types of chemical bonds: ionic, covalent, and metallic.

notes.title: Acids and Bases
notes.content: Acids are substances that donate hydrogen ions, while bases are substances that accept hydrogen ions. The pH scale is used to measure the concentration of hydrogen ions in a solution.

notes.title: Thermodynamics
notes.content: Thermodynamics is the study of heat and energy transfer. It includes the laws of thermodynamics, which describe how energy changes from one form to another.

notes.title: Kinetics
notes.content: Chemical kinetics is the study of the rates of chemical reactions. Factors that affect reaction rates include concentration, temperature, and surface area.

notes.title: Energetics
notes.content: Energetics is the study of the energy changes that occur during chemical reactions. It includes the concepts of exothermic and endothermic reactions.

facts.fact: The smallest atom is hydrogen, which has an atomic number of 1.
The largest atom is cesium, which has an atomic number of 55.
The heaviest naturally occurring element is uranium, with an atomic number of 92.
The lightest gas is hydrogen, which has a density of 0.0899 g/L.
The heaviest liquid is mercury, with a density of 13.546 g/cm³.
